description: Buffalo Creek & Gauley 2-8-0 steam locomotive 13 in front of the engine house in Dundon, West Virginia, the railroad's headquarters, in January 1964. The coal in the gondola at right is no doubt intended for the locomotive's firebox.
subject: Automobiles;Smoke & steam;Steam locomotives
rights: © Center for Railroad Photography and Art
notes: Buffalo Creek & Gauley 2-8-0 locomotive 13 was built by Alco in 1920 for the Kelly's Creek & Northwestern and acquired by BC&G in 1950.

title: Zimmermann-01-017-01
creator: Zimmermann, Karl
description: A railroad employee working on Buffalo Creek & Gauley 2-8-0 steam locomotive 13 at the railroad's headquarters in Dundon, West Virginia, on January 10, 1964.
subject: Railroad employees;Smoke & steam;Steam locomotives;Logging railroads
rights: © Center for Railroad Photography and Art

title: Zimmermann-01-016-11
creator: Zimmermann, Karl
description: Graham County Railroad Shay 1926 enters the Nantahala Gorge with its pop valves lifted, en route from Robbinsville to Topton, North Carolina, and interchange with the Southern Railway's Murphy Branch.
subject: Railroads–Curves and turnouts;Smoke & steam;Steam locomotives;Canyons
rights: © Center for Railroad Photography and Art
notes: In 1967, Shay 1926 was hauling tourist excursions as well as freight and the nickname “Old Sidewinder” had been inscribed on its tank, appropriate for its new role. The locomotive was numbered for the year it was built by Lima.

title: Zimmermann-01-013-10
creator: Zimmermann, Karl
description: Mobile & Gulf Railroad 2-6-0 steam locomotive 97 in Brownville, Alabama, in March 1967. Some poles lay on the ground near the locomotive.
subject: Railroad employees;Smoke & steam;Steam locomotives;Logs
rights: © Center for Railroad Photography and Art
notes: The Baldwin 2-6-0, originally meant to go to Cuba, was built in 1925 and came to the M&G in 1948. In Brownville, named for Brown Wood Processing, the creosoting plant that was the railroad's reason for being.
